park_id.note: Encoded to the integer category code = index of park_id in metadata.park_categories (the LightGBM training-time pd.Categorical order). The ONNX model takes this code as a float32 input; the park->code map is model-version-specific and lives in metadata, not this structural contract. Unknown park -> the booster's default-direction handling.

output.note: Decision [46] Option-A baseline: park-as-categorical (one 5-class distribution per (BIP, park) input row), NOT a per-park multi-output like the MLP. Per-class isotonic calibrators (calibrator.json, 5 of them) are applied to the raw softmax before serving. Co-registered comparator for battedball_outcome (decision [141]); LGBM edges Brier, MLP edges calibration.

description: Canonical Python<->Java feature contract for the Phase 2c LightGBM Option-A batted-ball baseline (batted_ball_lgbm_baseline): 16 input features (the MLP's 15 + park_id as a categorical) -> 5-class outcome distribution. Sibling of feature_pipeline_battedball.json (the MLP contract).
notes: Registry schema-hash check (rule 7) reads feature_order from here; the hash is recomputed over canonical JSON (sort_keys, no whitespace, schema_hash zeroed) by FeatureSchemaHasher (Java) and the .githooks/pre-commit Python snippet. ASCII-only so the Python and Java hashers agree. Trees do not z-score, so unlike the MLP contract there is no scaler. The 15 shared features must match training's features_shared.FEATURE_NAMES; park_id is appended (FEATURE_COLUMNS).
